headline: Here's How Much A Steve Jobs Autographed Apple II Manual Just Sold For
summary: Since his passing in 2011, Apple Inc (NASDAQ: AAPL) memorabilia featuring Steve Jobs has risen significantly in value. An auction ending Thursday night featured several Jobs related items that fetched some impressive valuations.

headline: Elon Musk Introduces Robots, Will Bill Gates Rally To Tax Them?
summary: One of the biggest news items of the week was the unveiling of the Tesla Bot, a humanoid robot to perform tasks, highlighted by Tesla Inc (NASDAQ: TSLA) CEO Elon Musk at the company’s AI Day.

headline: Here's Another Use Case Elon Musk Suggests For the Tesla Bot Announced At the EV Maker's AI Day
summary: Tesla, Inc.

headline: If You Had $5,000 Right Now, Would You Put It On Microsoft Or Facebook?

Every week, Benzinga conducts a survey to collect sentiment on what traders are most excited about, interested in or thinking about as they manage and build their personal portfolios.

This week we posed the following question to over 1,000 Benzinga visitors on tech investing:

headline: EV Week In Review: Tesla's 'AI Day' Front And Center, Nio On Damage Control Mode, Romeo's Rough Ride, Lordstown's 'Very Brief' Shareholder Meeting
summary: The bygone week was fairly calm on the EV front after weeks of a robust flow of news amid the earnings season. Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA)'s Elon Musk, however, provided the excitement that could last for the week and more, with the EV maker's AI Day presentation.

headline: Benzinga's Bulls And Bears Of The Week: Apple, Bitcoin, Microsoft, Robinhood, Walmart And More
summary:
Benzinga has examined the prospects for many investor favorite stocks over the past week. 
	
	Last week's bullish calls included big tech stocks and a rare earth materials producer.

headline: The Past Week's Notable Insider Buys Included AppLovin, Cricut, Biotechs And More
summary:
Insider buying can be an encouraging signal for potential investors, especially when markets are near all-time highs.
	
	Some insiders were making return trips to the buy window last week.
	
	Insider buying was also happening in the wake of recent earnings reports.

headline: The Latest Barron's Picks And Pans: Facebook, Jefferies, Palantir, Spotify And More
summary: This weekend's Barron's cover story examines the long-term prospects for big tech companies currently in the sights of regulators and lawmakers.

headline: Return On Capital Employed Overview: Alphabet
summary: Looking at Q2, Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) earned $19.36 billion, a 17.79% increase from the preceding quarter. Alphabet also posted a total of $61.88 billion in sales, a 11.87% increase since Q1. Alphabet earned $16.44 billion, and sales totaled $55.31 billion in Q1.

headline: Return On Capital Employed Overview: Tesla
summary: Looking at Q2, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) earned $1.31 billion, a 120.88% increase from the preceding quarter. Tesla also posted a total of $11.96 billion in sales, a 15.1% increase since Q1. In Q1, Tesla earned $594.00 million, and total sales reached $10.39 billion.

headline: Return On Capital Employed Overview: PayPal Holdings
summary: Looking at Q2, PayPal Holdings (NASDAQ:PYPL) earned $1.13 billion, a 8.16% increase from the preceding quarter. PayPal Holdings also posted a total of $6.24 billion in sales, a 3.4% increase since Q1. PayPal Holdings earned $1.04 billion, and sales totaled $6.03 billion in Q1.

headline: Netflix Insights: Return On Capital Employed
summary: Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) posted a 5.73% decrease in earnings from Q1. Sales, however, increased by 2.5% over the previous quarter to $7.34 billion. Despite the increase in sales this quarter, the decrease in earnings may suggest Netflix is not utilizing their capital as effectively as possible.

headline: Return On Capital Employed Overview: Apple
summary: Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) posted Q3 earnings of $24.13 billion, an increase from Q2 of 12.28%. Sales dropped to $81.43 billion, a 9.1% decrease between quarters. Apple earned $27.50 billion, and sales totaled $89.58 billion in Q2.
